channel.title: TechNode
channel.description: Latest news and trends about tech in China

item.title: BYD’s Yangwang U9 becomes first Chinese supercar in Gran Turismo 7
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Sony announced that BYD’s Yangwang U9 will be featured in Gran Turismo 7, making it the first Chinese-made supercar to appear in the PlayStation racing title. The U9 Xtreme recently set a Nürburgring lap record of 6:59.157, becoming the fastest production EV and the first to complete the track in under seven minutes. Back in […]
item.content:encoded: Sony announced that BYD’s Yangwang U9 will be featured in Gran Turismo 7, making it the first Chinese-made supercar to appear in the PlayStation racing title. The U9 Xtreme recently set a Nürburgring lap record of 6:59.157, becoming the fastest production EV and the first to complete the track in under seven minutes. Back in June, Gran Turismo developer Polyphony Digital announced a partnership with Xiaomi to bring the SU7 Ultra into the game, which is still under development and has yet to go live. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: China’s Moonshot AI reportedly raising several hundred million dollars in new funding round
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Chinese large model startup Moonshot AI is reportedly close to completing a new funding round worth several hundred million dollars, according to Chinese outlet Z Finance. The company has not yet responded to requests for comment. Moonshot AI last raised about $300 million in August 2024 at a post-money valuation of over $3.3 billion, with […]
item.content:encoded:
Chinese large model startup Moonshot AI is reportedly close to completing a new funding round worth several hundred million dollars, according to Chinese outlet Z Finance. The company has not yet responded to requests for comment.

Moonshot AI last raised about $300 million in August 2024 at a post-money valuation of over $3.3 billion, with investors including Alibaba and Tencent. The firm’s Kimi chatbot regained public attention in July with the launch of its upgraded Kimi k2 model, which drew strong reactions both in China and Silicon Valley after investors such as Chamath Palihapitiya and Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch publicly praised its performance. [Z Finance, in Chinese]

item.title: Alibaba launches preorders for its first in-house AI glasses, featuring AR navigation and visual payment integration across its ecosystem
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Alibaba has started preorders for its first self-developed _Quark AI Glasses_. The device is priced at ¥3,699 ($510) for members and ¥3,999 ($550) for regular buyers, with preorders now live on Tmall and deliveries expected in early December. Powered by Qualcomm’s AR1 and BES2800 dual flagship chips, the glasses integrate Alibaba’s ecosystem services, offering AR […]
item.content:encoded:
Alibaba has started preorders for its first self-developed _Quark AI Glasses_. The device is priced at ¥3,699 ($510) for members and ¥3,999 ($550) for regular buyers, with preorders now live on Tmall and deliveries expected in early December.

Powered by Qualcomm’s AR1 and BES2800 dual flagship chips, the glasses integrate Alibaba’s ecosystem services, offering AR navigation powered by AutoNavi Maps, secure visual payments through Alipay’s “Look” feature, visual product recognition on Taobao, and travel reminders from Fliggy, Alibaba’s travel service. It also features a SuperRaw night-mode system for clearer photos in low light. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: Huawei rolls out HarmonyOS 6, ahead of next month’s Mate 80 launch
item.dc:creator: Jessie Wu
item.description: On Wednesday, Huawei unveiled its next-generation operating system, HarmonyOS 6, and announced that the upcoming Mate 80 series will be the company’s first flagship smartphones to feature the new system. Centered on the themes of “smooth, intelligent, and secure,” the update leverages deep software-hardware integration to deliver an enhanced smart experience across devices.  As the […]
item.content:encoded:
On Wednesday, Huawei unveiled its next-generation operating system, HarmonyOS 6, and announced that the upcoming Mate 80 series will be the company’s first flagship smartphones to feature the new system. Centered on the themes of “smooth, intelligent, and secure,” the update leverages deep software-hardware integration to deliver an enhanced smart experience across devices. 

As the first devices to launch with HarmonyOS 6, the Mate 80 series are expected to debut by the end of November, possibly powered by the Kirin 9030 chipset.

Why it matters: The launch showcases Huawei’s development of HarmonyOS as a self-owned system independent of Android and iOS. The industry is curious about the performance of the Kirin 9030, as it could demonstrate Huawei’s latest chip innovation despite ongoing global restrictions.

Details: HarmonyOS 6 is the sixth major update to Huawei’s HarmonyOS since the first version, HarmonyOS 1, was launched in August 2019.

  • HarmonyOS 6 brings improved smoothness, with a 15% performance boost for devices upgrading from HarmonyOS 5 and a 40% boost for those upgrading from HarmonyOS 4, according to Huawei. Currently, over 23 million devices run HarmonyOS 5, and major apps such as WeChat have completed compatibility updates.
  • Huawei’s system AI assistant, Xiao Yi, has been upgraded to a proactive service model, capable of handling complex tasks with a single command, from travel planning and check-in to medical appointments. Supporting 16 dialects and image recognition for visually impaired users, Xiao Yi now powers over 80 HarmonyOS app AI assistants across healthcare, travel, and entertainment.
  • HarmonyOS 6 introduces enhanced privacy and security features, including proactive risk alerts that can automatically detect several types of potential scams during incoming calls. Users can also securely share files, and they can choose to restrict access to specific recipients or have the files self-destruct after a single view.

Context: The Huawei Mate 80 series is expected to launch in mid to late November, with sales beginning in early December. All models will come preinstalled with HarmonyOS 6 and  powered by the Kirin 9030 processor, featuring an active cooling fan system for the first time, according to MyDrivers. The lineup includes 6,000mAh silicon-carbon batteries, and some versions may also support 100W wired and 80W wireless fast charging.

item.title: Unitree Robotics partners with NetEase’s MMORPG to test robot technology in virtual worlds
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Chinese robotics company Unitree Robotics has partnered with NetEase’s martial arts MMORPG Justice Online Mobile to explore two-way integration between robotics and virtual environments. The collaboration will use the game’s world as a “digital proving ground” for Unitree’s robots, allowing user feedback to inform product development while enhancing in-game motion performance. Unitree’s robots will also […]
item.content:encoded: Chinese robotics company Unitree Robotics has partnered with NetEase’s martial arts MMORPG Justice Online Mobile to explore two-way integration between robotics and virtual environments. The collaboration will use the game’s world as a “digital proving ground” for Unitree’s robots, allowing user feedback to inform product development while enhancing in-game motion performance. Unitree’s robots will also assist with motion capture to improve realism and reduce injury risks during complex action scenes. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: ByteDance unveils Seed3D 1.0 model for high-fidelity 3D generation from a single image
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: ByteDance’s Seed team has launched Seed3D 1.0, a diffusion transformer-based model capable of generating simulation-grade 3D assets from a single image. The model produces detailed geometry, realistic textures, and physically based rendering (PBR) materials through large-scale data training. Seed3D 1.0 models can be directly imported into simulation platforms such as Nvidia Isaac Sim for embodied […]
item.content:encoded:
ByteDance’s Seed team has launched Seed3D 1.0, a diffusion transformer-based model capable of generating simulation-grade 3D assets from a single image. The model produces detailed geometry, realistic textures, and physically based rendering (PBR) materials through large-scale data training.

Seed3D 1.0 models can be directly imported into simulation platforms such as Nvidia Isaac Sim for embodied AI training, enabling complex scene construction with minimal adaptation. ByteDance said the model outperforms both open- and closed-source alternatives in texture, material, and geometry generation. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: Chinese smartphone maker Honor officially repositions as an AI-driven tech ecosystem company with new “Alpha Strategy”
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: At its AI Ecosystem Conference on Oct. 23, Honor Chairman Wu Hui said AI-powered intelligent agents will become “the next major interface for human-computer interaction,” adding that the company has invested over ¥10 billion ($1.37 billion) in AI R&D. Honor has officially repositioned itself from a smartphone maker to an AI-driven tech ecosystem company, outlining […]
item.content:encoded:
At its AI Ecosystem Conference on Oct. 23, Honor Chairman Wu Hui said AI-powered intelligent agents will become “the next major interface for human-computer interaction,” adding that the company has invested over ¥10 billion ($1.37 billion) in AI R&D. Honor has officially repositioned itself from a smartphone maker to an AI-driven tech ecosystem company, outlining a three-phase roadmap spanning smart devices, connected ecosystems, and an intelligent world under its new “Alpha Strategy.”

Product Line President Fang Fei detailed the company’s 1×3×N framework — one open platform, three key enablers, and multiple industry clusters. Through its HONOR AI Connect platform, Honor will open its IoT platform with free device connectivity and zero licensing fees, and make its 3,000 physical retail stores available to partners for sales and customer experience. The company also plans to expand its AI ecosystem globally, with an initial focus on Europe and Southeast Asia. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: DJI Osmo Action 6 leak reveals major upgrades including 8K video and smartwatch integration
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Leaked renders of DJI’s upcoming Osmo Action 6 show major upgrades in sensor size, video capability, and endurance, according to media report. The action camera reportedly features a 1/1.1-inch stacked CMOS sensor co-developed with Sony—nearly 40% larger than the previous generation—along with an F2.0–F4.0 variable-aperture 16mm lens. The device is said to support 8K/30fps HDR […]
item.content:encoded:
Leaked renders of DJI’s upcoming Osmo Action 6 show major upgrades in sensor size, video capability, and endurance, according to media report. The action camera reportedly features a 1/1.1-inch stacked CMOS sensor co-developed with Sony—nearly 40% larger than the previous generation—along with an F2.0–F4.0 variable-aperture 16mm lens.

The device is said to support 8K/30fps HDR and ProRes video, 5.3K/60fps, and 4K/120fps recording, with 4-hour battery life and IP68 water resistance up to 20 meters. Other highlights include built-in 64GB storage, RockSteady 4.0 stabilization, Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.1, and a new smartwatch link feature for remote control and real-time metrics. The camera is expected to launch by mid-November with a starting price of $329. [Igor Bogdanov, TheNewCamera]

item.title: Tencent’s WorldMirror 1.1 3D generation model enables single-GPU real-time deployment
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Tencent has released version 1.1 of its Hunyuan World Model, codenamed WorldMirror, adding support for multi-view and video inputs while enabling single-GPU deployment for real-time 3D scene creation. The company said the update allows users to generate 3D worlds in seconds. As a unified feedforward 3D reconstruction model, WorldMirror 1.1 expands beyond version 1.0’s text […]
item.content:encoded:
Tencent has released version 1.1 of its Hunyuan World Model, codenamed WorldMirror, adding support for multi-view and video inputs while enabling single-GPU deployment for real-time 3D scene creation. The company said the update allows users to generate 3D worlds in seconds.

As a unified feedforward 3D reconstruction model, WorldMirror 1.1 expands beyond version 1.0’s text and single-image inputs, now supporting multimodal priors such as camera and depth data with end-to-end 3D output. The model is fully open source on GitHub, with a Hugging Face Space available for online testing. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: China’s Noetix Robotics unveils $1,370 humanoid robot “Bumi”
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Chinese robotics startup Noetix Robotics has announced Bumi, the world’s first high-performance humanoid robot priced under ¥10,000 ($1,370). The 94 cm humanoid robot weighing 12 kg can walk on two legs and perform complex movements like dancing, marking the company’s entry into the consumer robotics market. Designed for education and home use, Bumi features lightweight […]
item.content:encoded:
Chinese robotics startup Noetix Robotics has announced Bumi, the world’s first high-performance humanoid robot priced under ¥10,000 ($1,370). The 94 cm humanoid robot weighing 12 kg can walk on two legs and perform complex movements like dancing, marking the company’s entry into the consumer robotics market.

Designed for education and home use, Bumi features lightweight composite materials, a self-developed motion control system, and an open programming interface for learning and creativity. Noetix said preorders will open between the Double 11 and Double 12 shopping festivals, calling the launch a milestone in bringing humanoid robots from labs to everyday life. [TechNode reporting]
